posts.name: Leo Biasiucci
posts.content: Thank you to everyone who joined our LD 30 constituent call tonight. Great questions on the budget and public safety—my office is following up with resources for each of you.

posts.name: Walt Blackman
posts.content: This week's committee work focused on rural broadband and school funding equity. I'll post a full recap once the amended bills are posted.

posts.name: Selina Bliss
posts.content: Honored to meet with local small business owners in Prescott today. Inflation and workforce shortages are still top concerns—we're pushing practical fixes, not more red tape.

posts.name: Michael Carbone
posts.content: Reminder: mobile office hours tomorrow in Lake Havasu City. If you need help with a state agency or want to talk legislation, stop by—no appointment needed.

posts.name: Neal Carter
posts.content: Proud to co-sponsor a bill that cuts unnecessary licensing delays for skilled trades. Arizona should be the easiest state to work and raise a family.

posts.name: Pamela Carter
posts.content: Quick update from the Capitol: the transportation package is moving. I'm focused on keeping dollars accountable and projects on schedule for our communities.

posts.name: Lupe Diaz
posts.content: Sierra Vista families—thank you for the thoughtful feedback on education choice and classroom safety. Your emails are shaping my amendments this session.

posts.name: Lisa Fink
posts.content: Water policy isn't flashy, but it's everything for Arizona's future. Grateful to stakeholders who spent time walking through the latest Colorado River modeling with us.

posts.name: John Gillette
posts.content: Border communities deserve consistent resources and clear communication from state partners. Today's briefing with sheriffs and mayors was productive—more to come.

posts.name: Matt Gress
posts.content: Housing supply and affordability remain urgent. I'm supporting reforms that speed up permitting where locals want growth while protecting neighborhoods.

posts.name: Gail Griffin
posts.content: Photos from the Cochise County Lincoln Day dinner are up on our page—thank you to every volunteer who makes grassroots work possible.

posts.name: Ralph Heap
posts.content: If you're navigating AHCCCS or DES benefits and hitting roadblocks, email the office. We can't promise outcomes, but we can get you a real person and a clear timeline.

posts.name: Laurin Hendrix
posts.content: East Valley parents: thanks for the school safety roundtable. Law enforcement, districts, and families all want the same thing—secure campuses and support for students.

posts.name: Rachel Keshel
posts.content: Energy reliability is a kitchen-table issue. Arizona should lead on an all-of-the-above strategy—without federal mandates that spike rates on working families.

posts.name: Alexander Kolodin
posts.content: Transparency matters: my office posts vote explanations and bill summaries weekly. If something is unclear, ask—I'd rather over-communicate than leave folks guessing.

posts.name: Nick Kupper
posts.content: Shout-out to our first responders training this weekend. Supporting mental health resources for firefighters and EMS isn't optional—it's overdue.
